old_claim.note: Hospital Information Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from medical_linear_accelerators to hospital_information_systems; the treatment machine is grounded in accelerator hardware and radiotherapy practice, not hospital IT.

source_shape.source_locator: AAPM Virtual Museum first therapy linac chronology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the first therapy linac as an 8 MeV accelerator installed at Hammersmith Hospital and first used on a patient in 1953.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This refutes HIS as a direct prerequisite because the 1953 treatment chronology and device description involve accelerator and radiotherapy infrastructure, not information systems.

ontology_before: Medical linacs depended on hospital information systems, mixing treatment-machine architecture with later administrative and workflow systems.
ontology_after: Medical linacs depend on particle acceleration plus radiotherapy clinical deployment context; hospital IT remains outside the direct method edge set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital information systems no longer appear as a direct medical-linac prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to modern radiotherapy information-management systems rather than medical linac machines.
A source showed the linac hardware itself requires hospital information-system infrastructure.
Removing HIS also removed the clinical radiotherapy context edge.
short_reason: Hospital information systems are not a medical linac hardware prerequisite.
